prompt: Extract all user-facing strings from a React/TypeScript web application and generate internationalization (i18n) resource files. The application source is at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/webapp/src/'. There are 25 component files in components/ directory, 10 page files in pages/ directory, 8 utility files in utils/ directory, and 5 hook files in hooks/ directory. Each file is 100-300 lines. Your task: 1) Read every source file in the project (48 files total). 2) Extract all user-facing strings: string literals in JSX text content, strings in title/placeholder/aria-label attributes, error messages and validation messages, toast/notification messages, button labels and link text, form labels and help text, alt text for images, any hardcoded text that a user would see. 3) For each extracted string, record: the string text, the file path, the line number, the component name, the context (button label, error message, heading, etc.). 4) Generate translation keys: create a consistent key naming convention (namespace.component.context), assign unique keys to all extracted strings, avoid key collisions, keep keys human-readable. 5) Generate the following resource files at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/webapp/i18n/locales/': en.json (English - the source strings), es.json (Spanish - with TODO markers for translation), fr.json (French - with TODO markers), de.json (German - with TODO markers), ja.json (Japanese - with TODO markers), zh.json (Chinese - with TODO markers). Each file should use the same key structure with English as the default for untranslated entries. 6) Generate a code migration guide at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/i18n-migration-guide.txt' with: total strings extracted, strings per component breakdown, suggested component refactoring patterns (showing before/after JSX), any strings that need manual context clarification, and a step-by-step guide to integrate the i18n library into the project. CRITICAL: This is for a global e-commerce platform in 50+ countries. You must also: 1) Handle RTL languages (Arabic, Hebrew) with proper text direction. 2) Support pluralization rules for all language families. 3) Generate locale files with fallback chains. 4) Create translation keys with semantic namespaces. 5) Validate no hardcoded strings remain using regex scanning.
